assertion_results.text: The response identifies that the Implementation Notes section is missing from the task description and flags this as a gap requiring clarification (constraint 1.6)
assertion_results.evidence: Gap #1 explicitly states: 'The task description lacks an Implementation Notes section. This section is a required part of the expected Jira task structure.' The response also explicitly cites constraint 1.6 in the opening paragraph explaining why execution is halted.

assertion_results.text: The response identifies that the Acceptance Criteria are vague — specifically calling out 'search results are relevant' and 'performance is acceptable' as insufficiently specific for implementation
assertion_results.evidence: Gap #2 explicitly calls out both 'Search results are relevant' (noting it is 'subjective and undefined', not pass/fail testable) and 'Performance is acceptable' (noting it is 'entirely subjective' with no quantitative threshold defined).

assertion_results.text: The response identifies that the Test Requirements are vague — 'test that search works correctly' does not define what 'correctly' means
assertion_results.evidence: Gap #3 directly quotes 'Test that search works correctly' and states: 'This does not define what "correctly" means. It does not specify which scenarios to test.'

assertion_results.text: The response asks the user for clarification rather than improvising implementation details — it does not proceed to write a full implementation plan (constraint 1.6)
assertion_results.evidence: The response explicitly states 'Execution is stopped' and 'I will not proceed until clarification is provided.' It poses clarifying questions (Q1-Q4) and defers implementation planning to after answers are received. No implementation plan or code is drafted.

assertion_results.text: The response asks at least 3 specific clarifying questions about: which fields to search, what relevance ranking means, and what performance threshold is required
assertion_results.evidence: Q1 asks which fields should be searchable (name, version, license, all fields). Q2 asks what 'relevant' means in terms of matching strategy and ranking (exact, ILIKE, full-text, existing SearchService). Q3 asks for a concrete performance threshold (response time, dataset size, indexing, caching). All three specific topics are covered by distinct questions.
